channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two young drivers face court after alleged street racing in Greenway
item.description: ACT Policing intercepted two 18-year-old men allegedly street racing in Greenway on Saturday night. The incident occurred around 11:10pm on Drakeford Drive, where police observed two Volkswagen Jetta ...

item.title: AI-Generated Sexual Deepfakes Affect Hundreds of Students Worldwide
item.description: A global review by WIRED and Indicator has revealed that AI-generated deepfake sexual images of students are a growing crisis in schools. Since 2023, at least 90 schools in 28 countries have reported ...

item.title: Vancouver Canucks find cohesion despite difficult NHL season
item.description: As the 2025-26 NHL season draws to a close, the Vancouver Canucks, anchored at the bottom of the standings, are experiencing a notable shift in team morale. Despite a challenging season marked by inju...

item.title: Balancing Heritage and Modernisation in Urban Development
item.description: Cities are more than just physical spaces; they are living archives that reflect human history through their streets, buildings, and neighbourhoods. In many parts of the world, urban areas are being r...

item.title: Southern states raise concerns over Lok Sabha seat expansion and delimitation plan
item.description: The Centre's proposal to expand Lok Sabha seats and implement one-third reservation for women has sparked a political debate, especially among southern states. The delimitation exercise will rely on p...

item.title: AA and BSM fined and required to refund learners over undisclosed booking fees
item.description: The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has ordered the AA Driving School and BSM Driving School to refund more than 80,000 customers and to pay a fine totalling £4.2 million after uncovering dece...

item.title: Fatal Pedestrian-Vehicle Collision in Mississauga Reported by Paramedics
item.description: On April 15, 2026, emergency services responded to a tragic traffic incident in Mississauga where a pedestrian was fatally struck by a vehicle. Paramedics attended the scene promptly, but the individu...

item.title: Victoria Police investigate Katy Perry following Ruby Rose sexual assault claims
item.description: Victoria Police have launched an investigation into international pop star Katy Perry following allegations of sexual assault made by Australian actor Ruby Rose. The claims have attracted significant ...

item.title: Iran-US indirect talks continue through Pakistan after Islamabad round ends without agreement
item.description: According to Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei, exchanges between Iran and the United States through Pakistani channel have continued even after the direct talks held in Islamabad over...

item.title: Kenya’s Maasai Mara Corridor Dispute Highlights Tension Between Conservation, Land Rights and Politics
item.description: The article explores how the blockage of wildlife corridors in Kenya’s Maasai Mara has become both an environmental and political issue. Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has claimed that illeg...

item.title: Tanzania National Swimming Championships: Crissa Dillip Sets New Records
item.description: Mambo! The 18th Tanzania National Club Swimming Championships just finished, na ilikuwa kali sana! Swimmers were breaking records left, right, and center. Crissa Dillip, from Taliss IST, was the star ...

item.title: Australian builders urge National Construction Code reform to ease rising costs amid housing pressures
item.description: Australia's construction industry is facing mounting costs due to global supply chain disruptions, particularly from the Middle East, affecting fuel and petrochemical plastics. Rising diesel prices an...

item.title: Retail Bathroom Access in Canada: Challenges and Safety Concerns at Winners, HomeSense, and Marshalls
item.description: Shoppers across Canada have reported that bathrooms in some Winners, HomeSense, and Marshalls locations have been closed or restricted. Individuals with medical conditions, like Stephanie Fraser from ...

item.title: Afghan villagers turn to gold panning in Kunar as livelihood amid job scarcity
item.description: In Afghanistan’s Kunar province, hundreds of men from remote villages spend their days digging and washing stones along the Kunar River in search of small traces of gold dust. Their work represents an...

item.title: Kuwaiti Authorities Detain Journalist Following Iran War Video Post
item.description: A journalist has reportedly been detained in Kuwait after sharing a video related to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Activists claim that the detention is linked to the journalist's online activity, whi...

item.title: Daniel MacPherson reflects on the challenges of filming Beast and pushing his limits
item.description: Daniel MacPherson has built his career on embracing challenges, often saying yes to roles that push him physically and mentally. From performing daring stunts in shows like Strike Back and Wild Boys t...

item.title: Key Cultural and Environmental Connections Between Finland and Canada
item.description: Finland and Canada, despite being on opposite sides of the Atlantic, share a variety of cultural, environmental, and societal similarities. Both countries have a strong passion for ice hockey, reflect...

item.title: Student Dies, Over 100 Fall Ill from Suspected Contaminated Meal at Odisha Government School
item.description: In a tragic incident at the Kakabandha Ashram School, a government-run Adivasi residential school in Mayurbhanj district, Odisha, a Class 5 student passed away and over 100 students fell ill after con...

item.title: Arizona Brandy releases new budots single 'Isaw' with DJ Love
item.description: Filipino drag performer and music artist Arizona Brandy has returned to the music scene with her latest single titled 'Isaw,' a track inspired by the popular budots dance movement. The single features...

item.title: Teenage student fatally shoots four in southeastern Turkiye school
item.description: In a tragic incident on Wednesday, a teenage student carried out a school shooting in Kahramanmaras province, southeastern Turkiye, resulting in the deaths of three students and one teacher, with at l...
